<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/introduction" rel="nofollow">Model Context Protocol</a> — Anthropic's open protocol for connecting agents to external tools, data sources, and services in a standardized way.</li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers">modelcontextprotocol/servers</a> — Anthropic's official reference MCP server implementations (GitHub, Slack, Postgres, Puppeteer, etc.). The authoritative source for understanding correct MCP server structure before building your own. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/15ad837a6c812b6d6aaf0655ea17891f797b5d364c03a9e06924c230998f87fb/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6d6f64656c636f6e7465787470726f746f636f6c2f736572766572733f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/15ad837a6c812b6d6aaf0655ea17891f797b5d364c03a9e06924c230998f87fb/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6d6f64656c636f6e7465787470726f746f636f6c2f736572766572733f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/modelcontextprotocol/servers?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp">microsoft/playwright-mcp</a> — Browser automation via accessibility tree snapshots rather than screenshots, dramatically reducing token cost. The canonical example of structured tool output design in an MCP server. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/3a8b2c2a919dc1506b8ed96e5472ff1ed506248dd910292e6662e995a3efef24/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6d6963726f736f66742f706c61797772696768742d6d63703f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/3a8b2c2a919dc1506b8ed96e5472ff1ed506248dd910292e6662e995a3efef24/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6d6963726f736f66742f706c61797772696768742d6d63703f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/microsoft/playwright-mcp?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/ChromeDevTools/chrome-devtools-mcp">Chrome DevTools MCP</a> — Official Google MCP server that exposes live Chrome debugging surfaces — network analysis, performance profiling, console messages, memory snapshots, and Lighthouse audits — as structured agent tools. The clearest reference for turning browser inspection into a first-class tool interface rather than relying solely on screenshot-driven automation. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent."><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent." alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/ChromeDevTools/chrome-devtools-mcp?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/callstackincubator/agent-device">agent-device</a> — MCP-native control layer for iOS and Android devices: snapshots, semantic targeting, typed client access, diagnostics, and replayable workflows. Fills a critical gap in the mobile-agent harness stack — most tool design assumes desktop or browser surfaces, but real-world agents increasingly need to interact with native mobile apps. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/9f682c6c52b2c0e6204dc304b45c775a2a2394496e47d96925bf578c60a8dafb/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f63616c6c737461636b696e63756261746f722f6167656e742d6465766963653f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/9f682c6c52b2c0e6204dc304b45c775a2a2394496e47d96925bf578c60a8dafb/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f63616c6c737461636b696e63756261746f722f6167656e742d6465766963653f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/callstackincubator/agent-device?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/a2aproject/A2A">A2A Protocol</a> — Google's open Agent-to-Agent protocol: JSON-RPC over HTTP(S)/SSE with Agent Card service discovery and a task/message/artifact communication model. The emerging standard for cross-framework agent interoperability in multi-agent harnesses. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/621c9ccc220fdba26c55de01069b8daf228690d6ac7a18c351c47b222f8bb269/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f61326170726f6a6563742f4132413f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/621c9ccc220fdba26c55de01069b8daf228690d6ac7a18c351c47b222f8bb269/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f61326170726f6a6563742f4132413f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/a2aproject/A2A?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/inspector">MCP Inspector</a> — Interactive debugging UI for MCP servers: inspect tool definitions, send test calls, and validate responses without wiring up a full agent. The essential development tool for anyone building or integrating MCP servers into a harness. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/60e0a7b71ceee9047772501de1fc318b4c0770eb1ba1ab50cbcbbd9d37cd5f2e/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6d6f64656c636f6e7465787470726f746f636f6c2f696e73706563746f723f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/60e0a7b71ceee9047772501de1fc318b4c0770eb1ba1ab50cbcbbd9d37cd5f2e/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6d6f64656c636f6e7465787470726f746f636f6c2f696e73706563746f723f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/modelcontextprotocol/inspector?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://developers.openai.com/blog/skills-shell-tips" rel="nofollow">Shell + Skills + Compaction: Tips for Long-Running Agents</a> — OpenAI's engineering guide to three production harness primitives: versioned Skill bundles (SKILL.md manifest; routing accuracy improved 73%→85% by adding negative examples), a managed shell container for durable tool execution, and server-side compaction via explicit <code>/responses/compact</code> endpoint. The most concrete first-party documentation of skills-based routing and compaction published in 2026.</li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/ComposioHQ/composio">Composio</a> — Wraps 250+ SaaS APIs (GitHub, Slack, Linear, Notion, etc.) as agent-ready actions with managed OAuth, so tool integration becomes a one-line import rather than a custom harness component per service. The fastest path from "the agent needs to call an external API" to a production-grade, authenticated tool. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/464c9f4f48693f6737fdfded814a04a4e8ebf5bdcd2f8be4d1bf1b9b28a8352d/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f436f6d706f73696f48512f636f6d706f73696f3f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/464c9f4f48693f6737fdfded814a04a4e8ebf5bdcd2f8be4d1bf1b9b28a8352d/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f436f6d706f73696f48512f636f6d706f73696f3f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/ComposioHQ/composio?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/transports" rel="nofollow">MCP Streamable HTTP Transport</a> — The transport that replaced HTTP+SSE in the 2025-11-25 spec, enabling MCP servers to run as remote services rather than local processes. Servers handle multiple client connections using HTTP POST (for client→server messages) and optional GET (for server→client SSE streams). The key harness architecture decision: Streamable HTTP unlocks remote MCP deployment but introduces session management complexity — stateful <code>Mcp-Session-Id</code> headers fight with load balancers and horizontal scaling, which the 2026 roadmap aims to resolve by decoupling sessions from the transport layer.</li>
<li><a href="https://blog.modelcontextprotocol.io/posts/2026-mcp-roadmap/" rel="nofollow">The 2026 MCP Roadmap</a> — The MCP team's roadmap for the next spec cycle: horizontal-scaling transport without stateful session constraints, <code>.well-known</code> discovery for capability advertisement without live connections, Tasks primitive with retry/expiry semantics, and enterprise extensions (audit trails, SSO, gateway behavior). Essential reading before investing heavily in MCP server infrastructure — the transport and discovery changes in particular affect how clients locate and connect to servers.</li>
<li><a href="https://developers.googleblog.com/en/developers-guide-to-ai-agent-protocols/" rel="nofollow">Developer's Guide to AI Agent Protocols</a> — Google's survey of six standardized agent interoperability protocols, each solving a distinct harness integration problem: MCP (tool/data connectivity), A2A (inter-agent routing via Agent Card discovery at well-known URLs), UCP (commerce workflows), AP2 (payment authorization with spend limits), A2UI (agent-driven dynamic UIs), AG-UI (streaming event format). The most practical map of which protocol to choose when an agent needs to cross a system boundary.</li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/ag-ui-protocol/ag-ui">AG-UI</a> — Lightweight event-driven protocol standardizing how AI agents connect to frontend applications: streaming state updates, tool call rendering, and HITL interrupts over a shared event bus. Fills the layer between MCP (tool access) and A2A (agent-to-agent) — it's the missing protocol for real-time agent-to-UI communication that neither MCP nor A2A was designed to address. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/f868b196989431b3c07f8812407ccf22e2d9410424b51eef2a990e046e8d2b8a/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f61672d75692d70726f746f636f6c2f61672d75693f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/f868b196989431b3c07f8812407ccf22e2d9410424b51eef2a990e046e8d2b8a/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f61672d75692d70726f746f636f6c2f61672d75693f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/ag-ui-protocol/ag-ui?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/code-execution-with-mcp" rel="nofollow">Code Execution with MCP: Building More Efficient Agents</a> — Anthropic's engineering account of reducing tool-call token overhead by having agents write code to interact with MCP servers rather than calling tools directly: up to 98.7% token reduction in experiments. Broadly applicable to any harness where tool schema overhead and intermediate results are consuming context — the pattern is to wrap multi-step tool interaction in a code execution primitive rather than exposing each operation as a discrete tool call.</li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/microsoft/skills">Microsoft Skills Framework</a> — Standardized framework for defining, versioning, and distributing agent skills. Enables skill reuse across Claude Code, Copilot, VS Code, Gemini, and other platforms — a harness-level abstraction that makes skills first-class deployment artifacts rather than ad-hoc tool definitions. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/5b51b5cbd712478f31fbedf3ae317b161c6e32174b8e8bbc83962d3f64adc7d8/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6d6963726f736f66742f736b696c6c733f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/5b51b5cbd712478f31fbedf3ae317b161c6e32174b8e8bbc83962d3f64adc7d8/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6d6963726f736f66742f736b696c6c733f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/microsoft/skills?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/skillmatic-ai/awesome-agent-skills">SkillNet & SkillsBench: Infrastructure for AI Agent Skills at Scale</a> — Comprehensive framework for creating, evaluating, and sharing agent skills with 86-task benchmark across 11 domains. Demonstrates the harness problem of skill fragmentation and provides infrastructure for standardized skill evaluation across frameworks. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent."><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent." alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/skillmatic-ai/awesome-agent-skills?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/03/amazon-bedrock-webrtc/" rel="nofollow">AWS Bedrock AgentCore with WebRTC Support</a> — Adds peer-to-peer, UDP-based WebRTC bidirectional streaming to Bedrock Agents for real-time voice interactions. Complements existing WebSocket support with lower latency and better resilience for poor network conditions. Essential harness-level transport choice for agents targeting sub-800ms Total Turn-Around Time voice interactions.</li>
<li><a href="https://juliangoldie.com/hermes-agent-unified-streaming/" rel="nofollow">Hermes Agent: Unified Streaming for Real-Time Agent Workflows</a> — Token-by-token streaming delivery system enabling real-time agent responses; sub-second decision loops on streaming events vs. batch-refreshed data. Critical infrastructure for harnesses where latency (not just throughput) is the constraint — agents must react to events as they arrive, not wait for batch completions.</li>
<li><a href="https://developers.googleblog.com/closing-the-knowledge-gap-with-agent-skills/" rel="nofollow">Google Developers: Closing the Knowledge Gap with Agent Skills</a> — Google ADK expansion with evaluation harness (117 prompts) for assessing skill performance across agentic coding, chatbots, document processing. Provides reference patterns and benchmark datasets for skill evaluation, complementing the Microsoft Skills Framework with Google's evaluation methodology.</li>
<li><a href="https://github.blog/ai-and-ml/github-copilot/whats-new-with-github-copilot-coding-agent/" rel="nofollow">What's New with GitHub Copilot Coding Agent</a> — GitHub's February 26, 2026 update is worth including for one specific reason: it makes <code>.github/agents/</code> custom agent files, self-review, built-in security scanning, and CLI handoff concrete as harness primitives rather than abstract ideas. Useful as a current reference for how repository-scoped agent definitions and security checks are being productized in a real coding-agent control plane.</li>
<li><a href="https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/ai-machine-learning/announcing-official-mcp-support-for-google-services" rel="nofollow">Announcing Official MCP Support for Google Services</a> — Google's 2026 rollout of managed MCP endpoints is a useful counterpoint to self-hosted MCP servers: discovery, IAM, audit logging, and Model Armor are provided as platform primitives instead of being rebuilt per server. Worth including because it shows what "enterprise MCP" looks like when the transport, auth, and governance layers are treated as product surface rather than glue code.</li>
<li><a href="https://devblogs.microsoft.com/powerplatform/dataverse-skills-your-coding-agent-now-speaks-dataverse" rel="nofollow">Dataverse Skills: Your Coding Agent Now Speaks Dataverse</a> — Microsoft's April 1, 2026 release is a strong concrete example of domain-specific skills done properly: the agent learns when to use MCP, when to drop to a Python SDK, and when to call a raw API, while the user stays in natural language. Worth adding because it shows that "skills" are not just prompt snippets, but curated execution strategies that hide a multi-tool integration stack behind intent.</li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/aws/agent-toolkit-for-aws">Agent Toolkit for AWS</a> — Official AWS-supported MCP servers, skills, and plugins that let AI agents provision, query, and manage AWS resources through a standardized protocol interface. Worth including as the reference for how a major cloud provider productizes infrastructure access into agent-ready harness primitives rather than leaving teams to hand-roll IAM-scoped tool definitions. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/c0680bbfc8a4e7ed18aa98ec31ea73e7ac409f1ae12d87ab882ec8504652e1c1/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6177732f6167656e742d746f6f6c6b69742d666f722d6177733f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/c0680bbfc8a4e7ed18aa98ec31ea73e7ac409f1ae12d87ab882ec8504652e1c1/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6177732f6167656e742d746f6f6c6b69742d666f722d6177733f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/aws/agent-toolkit-for-aws?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/codejunkie99/agentic-stack">agentic-stack</a> — Portable <code>.agent/</code> folder that externalizes memory, skills, and protocols from any specific coding agent into a cross-tool harness layer. Adapters translate the same configuration into Claude Code's <code>CLAUDE.md</code>, Cursor's rules, OpenCode's <code>AGENTS.md</code>, and more — the first practical answer to harness vendor lock-in at the configuration level. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/3654a836355d321f3ff817389aa913264cf2327c86718a82829978fff60b928b/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f636f64656a756e6b696539392f6167656e7469632d737461636b3f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/3654a836355d321f3ff817389aa913264cf2327c86718a82829978fff60b928b/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f636f64656a756e6b696539392f6167656e7469632d737461636b3f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/codejunkie99/agentic-stack?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/lastmile-ai/mcp-agent">mcp-agent</a> — Production-grade framework for building agents with MCP: composable workflows, built-in observability, and provider-agnostic model routing. The clearest reference for turning MCP servers from isolated utilities into a coherent agent harness. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/d88940054b29c46b123e560879b09b4c3a6c9b45f28922695c72cf4a9c4f6eb4/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6c6173746d696c652d61692f6d63702d6167656e743f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/d88940054b29c46b123e560879b09b4c3a6c9b45f28922695c72cf4a9c4f6eb4/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6c6173746d696c652d61692f6d63702d6167656e743f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/lastmile-ai/mcp-agent?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/vinkius-labs/vurb.ts">vurb.ts</a> — TypeScript framework for building production MCP servers with a "Presenter" perception layer that strips undeclared fields, redacts PII, and gates tool visibility by workflow state. Fills a critical gap in the MCP ecosystem: most tooling focuses on consuming servers, while vurb.ts addresses the harness engineering of authoring servers that are safe, governable, and context-aware by default. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/b2356f76f6ffe975efde150fe8e624aac0a94f31d881b5d17fbee1e6b85381c2/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f76696e6b6975732d6c6162732f767572622e74733f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/b2356f76f6ffe975efde150fe8e624aac0a94f31d881b5d17fbee1e6b85381c2/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f76696e6b6975732d6c6162732f767572622e74733f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/vinkius-labs/vurb.ts?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/microsoft/SkillOpt">SkillOpt</a> — Microsoft's skill optimizer that trains reusable natural-language skills for frozen LLM agents through trajectory-driven edits and validation-gated updates, producing deployable <code>best_skill.md</code> artifacts. The key harness insight is that skills should be treated as optimizable parameters that improve with execution feedback, not static prompt fragments written once and forgotten. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/7faa2d2baf48b900f3ff67344bfa8687f33a7e6b2722e715ea22344c384ee963/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6d6963726f736f66742f536b696c6c4f70743f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/7faa2d2baf48b900f3ff67344bfa8687f33a7e6b2722e715ea22344c384ee963/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6d6963726f736f66742f536b696c6c4f70743f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/microsoft/SkillOpt?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/sickn33/antigravity-awesome-skills">Antigravity Awesome Skills</a> — Installable library of 1,400+ agentic skills for Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, and more. The largest community-driven skill catalog with an npm installer and role-based bundles — a concrete reference for treating skills as versioned harness artifacts rather than ad-hoc prompts. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent."><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent." alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/sickn33/antigravity-awesome-skills?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/agentgateway/agentgateway">agentgateway</a> — Open-source agentic proxy that unifies LLM gateway, MCP gateway, and A2A gateway into a single control plane for managing multi-agent, multi-tool connectivity at scale. Provides drop-in security, observability, and governance for agent-to-LLM, agent-to-tool, and agent-to-agent communication — the missing infrastructure layer between agents and the services they touch. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/11b43125af88af194fe9444d016959fb976e672ffb4e68b9782a73ad9b8d4eed/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6167656e74676174657761792f6167656e74676174657761793f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/11b43125af88af194fe9444d016959fb976e672ffb4e68b9782a73ad9b8d4eed/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6167656e74676174657761792f6167656e74676174657761793f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/agentgateway/agentgateway?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.04781" rel="nofollow">AIP: A Graph Representation for Learning and Governing Agent Skills</a> — June 2026 proposal to replace free-form skill prose with directed execution graphs: discrete steps as nodes backed by deterministic scripts or natural-language descriptions, connected by explicit typed input/output edges and governed by a schema-validated YAML spec. Compiling skills to AIP improved Claude Sonnet's pass rate from 53% to 67% while making skills queryable, auditable, and repairable at the script level — the key harness advance is turning skill improvement from a prose rewrite into a measurable tuning loop.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Permissions & Authorization</h3><a id="user-content-permissions--authorization" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Permissions & Authorization" href="#permissions--authorization"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/beyond-permission-prompts" rel="nofollow">Beyond Permission Prompts</a> — Structured authorization patterns for agents: how to give agents the right permissions without relying on prompt-level trust.</li>
<li><a href="https://genai.owasp.org/llmrisk/llm062025-excessive-agency/" rel="nofollow">OWASP LLM06:2025 — Excessive Agency</a> — OWASP's authoritative definition of the "excessive agency" risk: over-provisioned functions, unnecessary permissions, and missing approval mechanisms. The standard checklist for auditing harness permission scope against principle of least privilege.</li>
<li><a href="https://wellarchitected.github.com/library/governance/recommendations/governing-agents/">GitHub Enterprise — Governing Agents</a> — April 2026 GitHub official guide for enterprise agent governance: MCP server registry curation with ruleset-protected configurations, agent environment standardization via <code>copilot-setup-steps.yml</code>, ephemeral runner enforcement, and cloud-agent firewall allowlisting. The most concrete published reference for governing agent fleets at scale without creating bottlenecks.</li>
<li><a href="https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/claude-code-auto-mode" rel="nofollow">Claude Code Auto Mode: A Safer Way to Skip Permissions</a> — Anthropic's engineering post on replacing approval fatigue (users approve 93% of prompts, making approvals meaningless) with a two-stage classifier: fast single-token gate first, chain-of-thought reasoning only on flagged actions. The design decisions — stripping assistant messages to prevent the agent from rationalizing dangerous actions, deny-and-continue recovery instead of halt — are the reference design for safe-by-default headless agent permissions.</li>
<li><a href="https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/permissions" rel="nofollow">Claude Agent SDK — Configure Permissions</a> — The most concrete reference for harness permission architecture: five-layer evaluation order (hooks → deny rules → permission mode → allow rules → canUseTool), <code>allowedTools</code>/<code>disallowedTools</code> declarative scoping, and four permission modes including <code>dontAsk</code> (deny-by-default for headless agents). The subagent inheritance warning for <code>bypassPermissions</code> alone is worth reading before any multi-agent deployment.</li>
<li><a href="https://blog.langchain.com/two-different-types-of-agent-authorization/" rel="nofollow">Two Different Types of Agent Authorization</a> — Distinguishes on-behalf-of authorization (agent uses end-user credentials, requires cross-channel identity mapping and per-user memory isolation) from fixed-credential authorization (agent owns its own account, requires human-in-the-loop guardrails on high-risk actions). The two models have fundamentally different threat surfaces and determine where authorization enforcement lives in the harness.</li>
<li><a href="https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/blog/microsoft-security-blog/authorization-and-governance-for-ai-agents-runtime-authorization-beyond-identity/4509161" rel="nofollow">Authorization and Governance for AI Agents: Runtime Authorization Beyond Identity at Scale</a> — Microsoft Security's reusable Authorization Fabric combining a Policy Enforcement Point (PEP) and Policy Decision Point (PDP) as a Microsoft Entra-protected endpoint. Every agent calls this fabric before tool execution, receiving a deterministic decision: ALLOW / DENY / REQUIRE_APPROVAL / MASK. Addresses the gap that identity alone (who is this agent?) doesn't answer whether a specific action should be executed now, by this agent, for this user, under the current business and regulatory context.</li>
<li><a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-klrc-aiagent-auth/" rel="nofollow">IETF draft-klrc-aiagent-auth: AI Agent Authentication and Authorization</a> — The first IETF standards-track specification for AI agent authentication (March 2026, authors from AWS, OpenAI, Zscaler, Ping Identity, Defakto Security). Builds on WIMSE (Workload Identity in Multi-System Environments) and OAuth 2.0 rather than inventing new protocols — agents get SPIFFE-style identifiers, with delegation via OAuth Token Exchange and DPoP for token binding. Essential reference for any harness that needs to authenticate agents across trust domains.</li>
<li><a href="https://nango.dev" rel="nofollow">Nango: Pre-Built Authentication for AI Agents</a> — Open-source platform providing pre-built OAuth and API key authentication for 700+ APIs across 30 categories. Automatically refreshes access tokens, provides webhooks when credentials break, and stores tokens securely so agent code never touches secrets. Solves the "agent needs to call an authenticated API" problem at scale — the authentication layer that complements Composio's tool wrapping. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/957a8cf8667f08b2eda3c49c8fcb4b5d73d01743c101bdca9c9a808a9427374d/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f4e616e676f48512f6e616e676f3f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/957a8cf8667f08b2eda3c49c8fcb4b5d73d01743c101bdca9c9a808a9427374d/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f4e616e676f48512f6e616e676f3f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/NangoHQ/nango?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.18491" rel="nofollow">AgentDoG: A Diagnostic Guardrail Framework for AI Agent Safety and Security</a> — Three-dimensional risk taxonomy (source/failure-mode/consequence) with fine-grained agentic safety benchmark (ATBench) and diagnostic guardrail models (4B–8B parameters) achieving 91.8% accuracy. Shifts safety monitoring from binary safe/unsafe checks to root-cause diagnosis: why did an action violate constraints? Where did the violation originate? What are the downstream consequences? Essential for production harnesses where transparency into safety decisions is required for audit trails.</li>
<li><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.20953" rel="nofollow">Open Agent Passport (OAP): Deterministic Pre-Action Authorization for Autonomous AI Agents</a> — March 2026 open specification and reference implementation that intercepts tool calls synchronously before execution, evaluates them against a declarative policy, and produces a cryptographically signed audit record. Enforces authorization in a median of 53ms; in a live adversarial testbed ($5,000 bounty), restrictive OAP policy achieved 0% attack success vs. 74.6% under permissive policies. Distinguishes pre-action authorization from sandboxed execution and model-based screening as complementary but distinct harness layers.</li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/manuelschipper/nah">nah</a> — Deterministic permission guard that maps tool calls to an intent taxonomy (<code>filesystem_delete</code>, <code>network_outbound</code>, <code>lang_exec</code>, etc.) rather than relying on command-name allow/deny lists. The key insight for harness design: the same binary can be benign or destructive depending on its arguments, so intent-level enforcement is the only reproducible safety layer. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/018316dd8fc0c368ca4bc67db1f8246ae8e34117705b40c8759daba4a13d0273/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6d616e75656c73636869707065722f6e61683f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/018316dd8fc0c368ca4bc67db1f8246ae8e34117705b40c8759daba4a13d0273/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6d616e75656c73636869707065722f6e61683f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/manuelschipper/nah?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Memory & State</h3><a id="user-content-memory--state" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Memory & State" href="#memory--state"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://www.anthropic.com/research/building-effective-agents" rel="nofollow">Building Effective Agents</a> — Covers in-context, external, and procedural memory patterns as harness-level concerns.</li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/letta-ai/letta">Letta (MemGPT)</a> — The reference architecture for stateful agents: three-tier memory (core / archival / recall) maps directly to harness state management design. Their <a href="https://www.letta.com/blog/letta-v1-agent" rel="nofollow">agent loop redesign post</a> is the most thorough public analysis of how memory structure shapes the harness. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/0eaa1349c8b560d75b3ca3b2b1b84a1d7ddae1371bf2a7cdf3cbd685dc625ea3/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6c657474612d61692f6c657474613f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/0eaa1349c8b560d75b3ca3b2b1b84a1d7ddae1371bf2a7cdf3cbd685dc625ea3/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6c657474612d61692f6c657474613f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/letta-ai/letta?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/mem0ai/mem0">mem0</a> — Drop-in universal memory layer (YC-backed, AWS Agent SDK's exclusive memory provider) that handles cross-session retention without custom harness-level state management code. Lowest integration cost for production-grade persistent memory. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/b963e7c8b678e4347d803314e3522f64330a417f6fa332a444b9dedbf4aacf32/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6d656d3061692f6d656d303f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/b963e7c8b678e4347d803314e3522f64330a417f6fa332a444b9dedbf4aacf32/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6d656d3061692f6d656d303f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/mem0ai/mem0?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/alash3al/stash">Stash</a> — Self-hosted persistent memory layer with an 8-stage consolidation pipeline (episodes → facts → relationships → patterns) and built-in MCP server. The critical gap it fills: production-grade cross-session memory without cloud dependencies or complex infrastructure — a single Docker Compose gives you Postgres, pgvector, and background consolidation. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/613ede5c2bfb1fe6303e8b05025e31e57ed53391f671ebb585712571906b8b30/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f616c61736833616c2f73746173683f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/613ede5c2bfb1fe6303e8b05025e31e57ed53391f671ebb585712571906b8b30/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f616c61736833616c2f73746173683f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/alash3al/stash?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/Tencent/TencentDB-Agent-Memory">TencentDB-Agent-Memory</a> — Tencent's fully local agent memory system with a 4-tier progressive pipeline (Conversation → Atom → Scenario → Persona) and symbolic short-term memory via Mermaid canvases. The benchmark data is striking: 61% token reduction and 51% relative pass-rate improvement on long-horizon tasks, demonstrating that hierarchical memory architecture outperforms flat vector stores for coding agents. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/8fa609ba81714b1ac86e3fa71a8ef6e505364a7db87c18f64dbd3f06f47d99a5/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f54656e63656e742f54656e63656e7444422d4167656e742d4d656d6f72793f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/8fa609ba81714b1ac86e3fa71a8ef6e505364a7db87c18f64dbd3f06f47d99a5/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f54656e63656e742f54656e63656e7444422d4167656e742d4d656d6f72793f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/Tencent/TencentDB-Agent-Memory?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/getzep/zep">Zep</a> — Purpose-built agent memory store with automatic conversation summarization, entity extraction, and semantic search over session history. Solves long-session context overflow at the memory layer rather than forcing the harness to manage trimming manually. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/194f9239424ce0b4fb2aaaeb61591b1cc91a3e87f2b3fa4107505845c5fd10ac/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6765747a65702f7a65703f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/194f9239424ce0b4fb2aaaeb61591b1cc91a3e87f2b3fa4107505845c5fd10ac/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6765747a65702f7a65703f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/getzep/zep?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/Gentleman-Programming/engram">engram</a> — Persistent memory for AI coding agents delivered as a single Go binary with SQLite + FTS5 and 18 MCP tools for save, search, session lifecycle, and conflict detection. The agent-agnostic, zero-dependency design makes cross-session memory a local harness primitive rather than a managed cloud service. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/9ea3e799e873dcaf31a2aecf1ad50a99d36430ed1e3af4f22d09102e0a09cdc1/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f47656e746c656d616e2d50726f6772616d6d696e672f656e6772616d3f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/9ea3e799e873dcaf31a2aecf1ad50a99d36430ed1e3af4f22d09102e0a09cdc1/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f47656e746c656d616e2d50726f6772616d6d696e672f656e6772616d3f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/Gentleman-Programming/engram?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/MemPalace/mempalace">MemPalace</a> — Local-first AI memory system that stores conversation history verbatim and retrieves it with semantic search through a structured palace architecture (wings, rooms, drawers). Achieves 96.6% R@5 on LongMemEval with zero LLM calls, making it the best-benchmarked open-source memory layer for agents that need cross-session persistence without cloud dependencies. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/4a50436af58399e1a68b5149696c050ad971c0e4527fbbec01a36bce3e00a495/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f4d656d50616c6163652f6d656d70616c6163653f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/4a50436af58399e1a68b5149696c050ad971c0e4527fbbec01a36bce3e00a495/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f4d656d50616c6163652f6d656d70616c6163653f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/MemPalace/mempalace?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/rohitg00/agentmemory">agentmemory</a> — Persistent memory layer purpose-built for coding agents with 95.2% retrieval accuracy and 92% token reduction, backed by real-world benchmarks. Its cross-agent architecture — one memory server serving Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and OpenCode through MCP and hooks — makes cross-session memory a portable harness primitive rather than a vendor-specific add-on. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/3ffae1a0e6095d04622eef94423bcb17d915e1086186f9520aa26d39cedca17e/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f726f6869746730302f6167656e746d656d6f72793f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/3ffae1a0e6095d04622eef94423bcb17d915e1086186f9520aa26d39cedca17e/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f726f6869746730302f6167656e746d656d6f72793f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/rohitg00/agentmemory?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/coleam00/claude-memory-compiler">claude-memory-compiler</a> — Turns raw Claude Code sessions into a self-evolving knowledge base: hooks capture every interaction, the Agent SDK extracts decisions and lessons, and an LLM compiler distills them into structured, cross-referenced articles that improve retrieval quality over time. The most concrete open-source implementation of trace-driven memory evolution for coding agents. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/7c03a591dbfbb6472eebf349f0f0e5c87d16875d50bcb4ba320bc76bf2612d7b/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f636f6c65616d30302f636c617564652d6d656d6f72792d636f6d70696c65723f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/7c03a591dbfbb6472eebf349f0f0e5c87d16875d50bcb4ba320bc76bf2612d7b/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f636f6c65616d30302f636c617564652d6d656d6f72792d636f6d70696c65723f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/coleam00/claude-memory-compiler?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://blog.langchain.com/how-we-built-agent-builders-memory-system/" rel="nofollow">How We Built Agent Builder's Memory System</a> — LangChain's engineering account of a COALA-based three-tier memory system (procedural/semantic/episodic) backed by PostgreSQL but exposed to agents as a virtual filesystem. Key harness decisions: human-in-the-loop approval gates every memory write (blocking prompt-injection via malformed writes), validation errors are fed back to the LLM for self-correction, and AGENTS.md serves as the agent's procedural memory anchor.</li>
<li><a href="https://github.blog/ai-and-ml/github-copilot/building-an-agentic-memory-system-for-github-copilot/" rel="nofollow">Building an Agentic Memory System for GitHub Copilot</a> — GitHub's January 15, 2026 write-up is one of the clearest public discussions of deployed cross-agent memory: repository-scoped memories are shared across coding agent, CLI, and code review, but only after just-in-time verification against the current code state. The core harness lesson is that memory quality is mostly a freshness and invalidation problem — stale, branch-specific memories are often more dangerous than having no memory at all.</li>
<li><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.18330" rel="nofollow">MemArchitect: A Policy-Driven Memory Governance Layer</a> — Proposes a governance layer that decouples memory lifecycle management (decay, conflict resolution, privacy enforcement) from model weights, directly addressing the "zombie memory" problem: outdated facts sitting in the context window that only a harness-level eviction policy — not the model — can remove.</li>
<li><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.20478" rel="nofollow">Codified Context: Infrastructure for AI Agents in a Complex Codebase</a> — Production-validated architecture (283 sessions, 108k-line codebase) built on three components: a "hot-memory constitution" encoding conventions and multi-agent coordination protocols, 19 domain-specialist agents, and a "cold-memory knowledge base" of 34 on-demand specification documents. The empirical data distinguishes what must live in always-on context from what should be retrieved on demand — the most concrete published guidance for scaling cross-session memory in a large codebase.</li>
<li><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17781" rel="nofollow">Facts as First Class Objects: Knowledge Objects for Persistent LLM Memory</a> — Identifies three production failure modes of in-context memory at scale: capacity overflow at ~8,000 facts, 60% fact destruction during compaction, and 54% behavioral drift from constraint erosion across cascaded summarizations. Proposes Knowledge Objects (hash-addressed discrete fact tuples) achieving 100% accuracy at 252× lower cost than in-context storage — the quantitative case for moving persistent facts out of the context window into a structured retrieval layer rather than managing them through prompt engineering.</li>
<li><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.22352" rel="nofollow">Recoverability Has a Law: The ERR Measure for Tool-Augmented Agents</a> — Formal framework for measuring how well agents recover from tool failures. Defines Expected Recovery Regret (ERR) as a metric for harness design: the cost of recovering from stochastic failures in downstream tasks. Critical for assessing reliability of production harnesses where tool calls occasionally fail but agents must continue functioning.</li>
<li><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.03236" rel="nofollow">MAGMA: Multi-Graph Agentic Memory Architecture</a> — Represents agent memory across four orthogonal semantic, temporal, causal, and entity graphs, enabling policy-guided retrieval over relational views. Outperforms MemGPT on long-horizon reasoning benchmarks by 18.5% accuracy improvement. The multi-graph abstraction lets harness engineers compose different retrieval strategies for different task phases — a concrete architecture for memory that scales beyond single-view approaches.</li>
<li><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.27910" rel="nofollow">GAAMA: Graph Augmented Associative Memory for Agents</a> — Hybrid memory system blending graph traversal with semantic similarity through additive scoring; graph augmentation improves retrieval over embedding-only approaches for long-horizon reasoning. Practical alternative to full multi-graph systems when adding structure to existing vector-based memory is sufficient.</li>
<li><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17244" rel="nofollow">Graph-Native Cognitive Memory for AI Agents: Formal Belief Revision Semantics for Versioned Memory Architectures</a> — Formal semantics for versioned memory graphs with belief revision operations, enabling agents to maintain coherent evolving world models through multi-turn reasoning. Addresses the hard problem of inconsistency resolution in long-lived agent memory: when new information contradicts prior beliefs, how should the agent update its knowledge base?</li>
<li><a href="https://blog.langchain.com/continual-learning-for-ai-agents/" rel="nofollow">Continual learning for AI agents</a> — LangChain's April 2026 framing of agent learning as three distinct layers: model weights, harness behavior, and contextual memory. Essential for designing memory systems that don't just store facts but actually improve agent performance over time through trace-driven harness and context updates.</li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/topoteretes/cognee">cognee</a> — Open-source memory platform with a hybrid graph-vector-relational poly-store that lets agents recall facts through both semantic similarity and structured graph traversal — the practical middle ground between flat vector stores and full multi-graph research systems. The self-improving pipeline reweights edges from agent feedback, and the 14-tool MCP server makes it a drop-in memory primitive for production harnesses. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/f99465218379e968ebb12150c0d673df0d4bc17106c7859d2d7d8de2d70dcda5/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f746f706f746572657465732f636f676e65653f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/f99465218379e968ebb12150c0d673df0d4bc17106c7859d2d7d8de2d70dcda5/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f746f706f746572657465732f636f676e65653f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/topoteretes/cognee?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Task Runners & Orchestration</h3><a id="user-content-task-runners--orchestration" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Task Runners & Orchestration" href="#task-runners--orchestration"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://openai.com/index/harness-engineering/" rel="nofollow">Harness Engineering</a> — How task runners fit into the harness: queueing, parallelism, and progress reporting.</li>
<li><a href="https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-c-compiler" rel="nofollow">Building a C Compiler with a Team of Parallel Claudes</a> — Anthropic's account of coordinating 16 Claude instances in parallel on a shared git repo without a central orchestrator: agents claim tasks via files in <code>current_tasks/</code>, git forces collision resolution naturally, and a continuous restart loop spawns fresh sessions that resume where predecessors left off. Key harness lesson: verbose test output pollutes agent context — the feedback loop must emit only a few summary lines, log detail to file.</li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm">LiteLLM</a> — Unified proxy and SDK that routes to 100+ LLM providers behind a single OpenAI-compatible interface, with a Router handling retry/fallback across deployments, per-project cost and rate-limit tracking, and OTEL callback integrations. The right infrastructure layer when your harness needs provider resilience (automatic failover on 429/500 errors), budget guardrails, or the ability to swap models without touching orchestration code. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/c22798b27ba1e00fbf5ec10b4bb8f37903b5cfa27c37d74668d90dc1e1ee4dbc/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f426572726941492f6c6974656c6c6d3f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/c22798b27ba1e00fbf5ec10b4bb8f37903b5cfa27c37d74668d90dc1e1ee4dbc/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f426572726941492f6c6974656c6c6d3f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/BerriAI/litellm?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/langchain-ai/langgraph">LangGraph</a> — Graph-based state machine framework for multi-agent harnesses: models supervisor/subagent topologies, error-recovery branches, and checkpoint persistence as first-class primitives. The most widely adopted harness orchestration layer in production. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/c6c2bd861e48586569c1b6e63388caeed9191b7168839bd117a48197fe67bc78/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6c616e67636861696e2d61692f6c616e6767726170683f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/c6c2bd861e48586569c1b6e63388caeed9191b7168839bd117a48197fe67bc78/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6c616e67636861696e2d61692f6c616e6767726170683f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/langchain-ai/langgraph?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/openai/openai-agents-python">OpenAI Agents SDK</a> — Lightweight multi-agent framework built around handoffs and guardrails; the production successor to Swarm. <li><a href="https://github.com/openai/symphony">Symphony</a> — OpenAI's orchestration layer for moving from supervising coding agents to managing work: it monitors an issue tracker, creates isolated per-issue workspaces, and surfaces proof-of-work artifacts (CI status, review feedback, walkthrough videos) as the handoff signal. The spec deliberately leaves sandbox and approval policies implementation-defined so teams can match trust posture to environment, and the in-repo <code>WORKFLOW.md</code> pattern versions agent runtime policy alongside code. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/7ae0ece422740675861b762114a098c6c32882eb972d3086cf5930596e4c0ab5/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6f70656e61692f73796d70686f6e793f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/7ae0ece422740675861b762114a098c6c32882eb972d3086cf5930596e4c0ab5/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6f70656e61692f73796d70686f6e793f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/openai/symphony?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/GammaLabTechnologies/harmonist">Harmonist</a> — Drop-in multi-agent framework where protocol enforcement is a mechanical gate, not a prompt request: IDE-level hooks check every code-changing turn for required reviewers, memory updates, and supply-chain integrity before the turn can complete. Built on stdlib Python with zero dependencies, it demonstrates how to build deterministic harness constraints that even frontier models cannot override. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/cacdca9cca2900b29f69e06daf197fcdc436f8d31c34af3d0115adb0d62c4505/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f47616d6d614c6162546563686e6f6c6f676965732f6861726d6f6e6973743f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/cacdca9cca2900b29f69e06daf197fcdc436f8d31c34af3d0115adb0d62c4505/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f47616d6d614c6162546563686e6f6c6f676965732f6861726d6f6e6973743f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/GammaLabTechnologies/harmonist?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/aden-hive/hive">Hive</a> — YC-backed production harness that compiles multi-agent objectives into deterministic execution DAGs with state persistence, crash recovery, cost enforcement, and human-in-the-loop oversight. The most complete open-source reference for running agent workloads in production rather than demos. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/939ed6b14c15969a792e11cb4eae5e88af99655328d11652be5e52936971a01c/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6164656e2d686976652f686976653f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/939ed6b14c15969a792e11cb4eae5e88af99655328d11652be5e52936971a01c/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6164656e2d686976652f686976653f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/aden-hive/hive?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/thClaws/thClaws">thClaws</a> — Native-Rust agent harness platform with four surfaces (GUI, CLI, web, one-shot), multi-provider routing, and sovereign-by-design architecture. The most complete open-source reference for a locally-run, offline-capable harness that unifies MCP, skills, AGENTS.md, hooks, and session resumption in a single binary. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/e833e3d2ffa8dbb2611805f01ca8d243c280e0992cdac635debdd552858caf3f/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f7468436c6177732f7468436c6177733f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/e833e3d2ffa8dbb2611805f01ca8d243c280e0992cdac635debdd552858caf3f/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f7468436c6177732f7468436c6177733f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/thClaws/thClaws?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/mattpocock/sandcastle">sandcastle</a> — TypeScript-native orchestration for sandboxed coding agents that treats provider-agnostic isolation (Docker, Podman, or Vercel Firecracker microVMs) as a primitive, not a framework. The built-in review-pipeline and parallel-AFK-agent patterns demonstrate how lightweight harness layers can enforce safety without the complexity of full graph orchestration. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/9a45464c7421bc44eb76908c0812261d4bf0d7c52c31ce795254d3e0476248e1/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6d617474706f636f636b2f73616e64636173746c653f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/9a45464c7421bc44eb76908c0812261d4bf0d7c52c31ce795254d3e0476248e1/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f6d617474706f636f636b2f73616e64636173746c653f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/mattpocock/sandcastle?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/sipyourdrink-ltd/bernstein">bernstein</a> — Deterministic scheduler for 40+ CLI coding agents running in parallel git worktrees with an HMAC-signed audit chain, signed agent cards, and per-artefact lineage. The zero-LLM coordination loop and tamper-evident audit trail make it the only open-source orchestrator designed for compliance-sensitive agent fleets. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/b7bdd5468626594c8d1391622b734b0435874b199574ea71ce92c6550e8ff8dc/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f736970796f75726472696e6b2d6c74642f6265726e737465696e3f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/b7bdd5468626594c8d1391622b734b0435874b199574ea71ce92c6550e8ff8dc/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f736970796f75726472696e6b2d6c74642f6265726e737465696e3f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/sipyourdrink-ltd/bernstein?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Verification & CI Integration</h3><a id="user-content-verification--ci-integration" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Verification & CI Integration" href="#verification--ci-integration"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/demystifying-evals-for-ai-agents" rel="nofollow">Demystifying Evals for AI Agents</a> — How to build verification into the harness loop, not just as a post-hoc eval.</li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/promptfoo/promptfoo">promptfoo</a> — YAML-driven LLM testing framework with LLM-as-judge, assertion DSL, and native CI integration. The most practical tool for adding agent output regression tests to a PR pipeline without writing a test harness from scratch. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/250d4a29e2b879c0a5072147377f970511574fd8258f2619eff7df9928f9510d/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f70726f6d7074666f6f2f70726f6d7074666f6f3f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/250d4a29e2b879c0a5072147377f970511574fd8258f2619eff7df9928f9510d/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f70726f6d7074666f6f2f70726f6d7074666f6f3f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/promptfoo/promptfoo?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/THUDM/AgentBench">AgentBench</a> — Multi-environment agent benchmark (OS, DB, web, code) with a structured eval pipeline. Worth studying for its environment isolation design and task definition format when building custom eval environments for your harness. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/de1a21b5c852dc2df90cb2573e1e1182c4c2207aefa8819c0626e3b2309ba9b8/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f544855444d2f4167656e7442656e63683f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/de1a21b5c852dc2df90cb2573e1e1182c4c2207aefa8819c0626e3b2309ba9b8/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f544855444d2f4167656e7442656e63683f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/THUDM/AgentBench?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://developers.openai.com/blog/eval-skills" rel="nofollow">Testing Agent Skills Systematically with Evals</a> — OpenAI's framework for skill regression testing: four eval dimensions (outcome, process, style, efficiency goals), JSONL trace capture for deterministic checks (command sequences, token budgets, repo cleanliness), then rubric-based grading only where deterministic checks don't suffice. The layering principle — add expensive LLM-as-judge checks only where they reduce meaningful risk — is the most actionable published guide to CI pipelines for agent skills that don't collapse under eval cost.</li>
<li><a href="https://blog.langchain.com/agent-evaluation-readiness-checklist/" rel="nofollow">Agent Evaluation Readiness Checklist</a> — A 33-item checklist covering the full evaluation lifecycle: error taxonomy, three-level granularity (single-step → trace → multi-turn thread), grader specialization, and CI integration. Key insight: capability evals (low pass rate, improvement target) and regression evals (near-100%, protection target) must be separated — mixing them produces wrong prioritization decisions.</li>
<li><a href="https://blog.langchain.com/evaluating-skills/" rel="nofollow">Evaluating Skills</a> — LangChain's methodology for benchmarking agent skills in Docker-sandboxed environments. Key empirical findings: Claude Code achieved 82% task completion with curated skills vs. 9% without, and consolidating to ≤12 skills improved accuracy over sprawling skill sets. The baseline-vs-skills comparison design with bugfix tasks and clear outcome metrics is the template for systematic skill coverage testing.</li>
<li><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.02601" rel="nofollow">AgentAssay: Token-Efficient Regression Testing for Non-Deterministic Agent Workflows</a> — Addresses agent CI's core problem: binary pass/fail is useless for non-deterministic workflows. Behavioral fingerprinting detects 86% of regressions vs. 0% with binary testing; stochastic PASS/FAIL/INCONCLUSIVE verdicts grounded in hypothesis testing cut token costs 78%. Trace-first offline mode runs regression checks against production traces at zero additional inference cost.</li>
<li><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.20075" rel="nofollow">Agentic Harness for Real-World Compilers: A Case Study in Specialized Tool Design</a> — Demonstrates how harness specialization (llvm-autofix) for a narrow domain (compiler bug fixes) achieves better results than general-purpose coding agents. The tool design patterns — exposing compiler error messages directly, bounding search depth by compilation cost — are transferable to any domain where cost-based pruning and specialized feedback loops make the difference between failure and success.</li>
<li><a href="https://developers.redhat.com/articles/2026/03/23/eval-driven-development-build-evaluate-ai-agents" rel="nofollow">Eval-Driven Development: Build and Evaluate Reliable AI Agents</a> — Red Hat's eight-stage evaluation maturity progression from manual CLI testing to cost-aware continuous monitoring (March 2026). Uses DeepEval with 15 custom <code>ConversationalGEval</code> metrics and LLM-as-judge; key finding: evaluator model capability matters significantly — llama-3-3-70b caught all known failures while smaller models missed 4–5 cases. The $0.64/run cost estimate and self-hosted evaluator pattern on OpenShift AI provide concrete guidance for teams building eval harnesses under real budget constraints.</li>
<li><a href="https://galileo.ai/blog/agent-evaluation-framework-metrics-rubrics-benchmarks" rel="nofollow">Agent Evaluation Framework 2026: Metrics, Rubrics & Benchmarks</a> — Comprehensive framework combining multi-environment baselines (AgentBench), domain-specific benchmarks (Terminal Bench 2.0, WebArena, SWE-bench Verified), and industry standards (NIST AI Agent Standards Initiative, February 2026). Provides reference metrics and rubrics for evaluating coding agents, chatbots, and specialized agents across dimensions (correctness, efficiency, safety). Essential for building eval harnesses that measure across standardized dimensions.</li>
<li><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.17753" rel="nofollow">The 2025 AI Agent Index: Documenting Technical and Safety Features of Deployed Agentic AI Systems</a> — Systematic index analyzing deployed agent safety documentation, guardrails, and third-party testing across 30 production systems; identifies critical gaps in agentic safety disclosure and documentation. Useful as a checklist for production harness safeguards before deployment.</li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/sentrux/sentrux">sentrux</a> — Real-time architectural sensor that closes the feedback loop for coding agents: scans codebases, scores structural health, and surfaces degradation via MCP so agents can self-correct before entropy compounds. The <code>gate --save</code> / <code>gate</code> pair makes architectural regression detection CI-friendly — filling the verification gap between linters (style) and tests (behavior) for code produced by autonomous harnesses. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/8897a83b85865f452ab0a82b36396d1a8690399709bc580d451a02bca40bf770/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f73656e747275782f73656e747275783f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/8897a83b85865f452ab0a82b36396d1a8690399709bc580d451a02bca40bf770/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f73656e747275782f73656e747275783f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/sentrux/sentrux?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Observability & Tracing</h3><a id="user-content-observability--tracing" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Observability & Tracing" href="#observability--tracing"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://github.com/traceloop/openllmetry">OpenLLMetry</a> — OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation for LLM calls and agent steps: adds trace spans to every inference and tool call without modifying business logic. The cleanest way to bring the existing OTEL ecosystem (Grafana, Datadog, Jaeger) to a harness. <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/0dea75e3017d1ecc29186a6ff81d30bc198d9f68af307faef03aab3dee508d56/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f74726163656c6f6f702f6f70656e6c6c6d657472793f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/0dea75e3017d1ecc29186a6ff81d30bc198d9f68af307faef03aab3dee508d56/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f74726163656c6f6f702f6f70656e6c6c6d657472793f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d25453225393825383526636f6c6f723d79656c6c6f77" alt="Stars" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/traceloop/openllmetry?style=flat-square&label=%E2%98%85&color=yellow" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/Arize-ai/phoenix">Arize Phoenix</a> — Self-hostable trace UI and eval runtime for agent workflows.
